title: Add eBPF snooper that traces the entire fork/exec graph of podman
body:
Added fork/exec snooper that can be used to facilitate performance tracing especially of the startup of Podman. It is based on the work @aktau did in the original [forkexecsnoop.bt](https://github.com/aktau/dotfiles/blob/f7a035f9fab0683bf449851058a46481ef998a38/bpftrace/forkexecsnoop.bt#LL1) which is Apache 2 licenced as can be seen [here](https://github.com/aktau/dotfiles/commit/f7a035f9fab0683bf449851058a46481ef998a38).

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: @Luap99 This function has been added in the very latest release of bpftrace (https://github.com/iovisor/bpftrace/issues/2497) so I believe you are using an older version.

> @Luap99 This function has been added in the very latest release of bpftrace ([iovisor/bpftrace#2497](https://github.com/iovisor/bpftrace/issues/2497)) so I believe you are using an older version.

If podman is always the first part, the older `strncmp()` could be used too, as [in the reference](https://github.com/iovisor/bpftrace/blob/master/docs/reference_guide.md#19-strncmp-compare-first-n-characters-of-two-strings):

```
if (strncmp("podman", comm, 6) == 0) { /* ... */ }
```

It was added in [commit a1d0263737b44e02aed37bee52dd99870d120f86](https://github.com/iovisor/bpftrace/commit/a1d0263737b44e02aed37bee52dd99870d120f86) in November 2019.

> > @Luap99 This function has been added in the very latest release of bpftrace ([iovisor/bpftrace#2497](https://github.com/iovisor/bpftrace/issues/2497)) so I believe you are using an older version.
> 
> If podman is always the first part, the older `strncmp()` could be used too, as [in the reference](https://github.com/iovisor/bpftrace/blob/master/docs/reference_guide.md#19-strncmp-compare-first-n-characters-of-two-strings):
> 
> ```
> if (strncmp("podman", comm, 6) == 0) { /* ... */ }
> ```
> 
> It was added in [commit a1d0263737b44e02aed37bee52dd99870d120f86](https://github.com/iovisor/bpftrace/commit/a1d0263737b44e02aed37bee52dd99870d120f86) in November 2019.

I guess this should work.

Anyway my comment wasn't meant to block this, I was just testing it. It is not like I have any need for this script and I will update to f38 anyway soon so this is a non issue there.

> f38 anyway soon so this is a non issue there.

If you use bluetooth audio, be prepared to restart pipewire _a lot_ :^)

Let's try the `strncmp` for older versions and then we can merge.

@aktau Thanks for the hint.
@vrothberg I have adjusted the file accordingly.

That fix seems to work but unfortunately it still fails on f37:

            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

            curtask->pid,
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~

            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

            ~~~~~~~~~~~~

            curtask->parent->pid,
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

            curtask->tgid,
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:112:13-28: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'
            curtask->parent->tgid,
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:131:13-25: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'

            ~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:132:13-28: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'
            curtask->parent->pid,
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:133:13-26: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'

            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:157:13-25: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'
            curtask->pid,
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:158:13-28: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'
            curtask->parent->pid,
            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:159:13-26: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'

            ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./hack/fork_exec_snoop.bt:160:13-28: ERROR: Unknown struct/union: 'struct task_struct'

```

Anyway as said before I am not blocking it. If it only works on newer systems that is fine for me.

Thanks for checking, @Luap99.

OK, then let's get it in as is.  Thanks, @raballew !
